mandates.mandate: The Conductor never executes workflow steps itself. It routes and dispatches.
Phase detection rules are evaluated in the exact order listed. First match wins.
The Conductor's sidecar is privileged — it persists across sessions and accumulates routing intelligence.
YOLO interaction mode is passed through to the workflow engine, not consumed by the Conductor.
The Conductor presents routing decisions transparently. No silent routing.
User can always override by naming a specific workflow.

advisory-patterns.description: Keyword hints for intent extraction. Advisory — AI applies judgment for ambiguous cases.

pattern.keywords: fix, debug, error, trace, stack, broken, crash
small, quick, hotfix, tweak, minor, patch
create, build, write, design, draft, new

step.title: Receive user goal
step.action: Receive the user's natural-language goal from the /symphony command invocation.
If the user named a specific workflow (e.g., "/symphony-dev-story"), skip to step 6 with that workflow. Confidence = 1.0 (user-explicit).

step.title: Parse intent
step.action: Extract from the user's goal: intent verb, target noun, scope hint.
Use the advisory-patterns above as guidance. Match the goal's language against pattern keywords.
If multiple patterns match, prefer the more specific one. If ambiguous, note both candidates for the confidence gate.
Record: parsed_intent = {verb, noun, scope}.

step.title: Detect phase
step.action: Evaluate the following 9 rules in order. First match wins.
step.rule: Route to diagnose-then-fix path. Skip remaining rules. This bypasses phase detection.
Route to Overture workflow. Entry mode: existing codebase.

rule.trigger: Source code exists at project_path AND no Symphony planning artifacts exist (no docs/planning-artifacts/, no docs/implementation-artifacts/)

step.rule: Route to Overture path (auto-detected). This prevents users with existing codebases from being dumped into Phase 1. User can override at confidence gate.

rule.trigger: Intent matches quick pattern AND approved architecture exists (docs/planning-artifacts/architecture.md)

step.rule: Route to quick path: quick-spec followed by quick-dev.

rule.trigger: Product-brief exists but no PRD (no docs/planning-artifacts/prd.md)
PRD exists but no architecture (no docs/planning-artifacts/architecture.md)
Architecture exists AND sprint is active (docs/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.md exists with status: active)
All stories have status done AND no release shipped (no docs/implementation-artifacts/release-plan.md or status: pending)

step.rule: Present all 5 phases to the user. Ask which to enter. Wait for response.

step.title: Classify complexity
step.action: Based on the parsed intent, scope hint, and artifact count, classify the task:
simple — single file, well-known pattern, quick keywords, small scope. Suggest quick path if architecture exists.
medium — multiple files, moderate scope, some unknowns. Standard workflow.
complex — multiple systems, unfamiliar domain, security-critical, high integration risk. Full workflow, consider parallel-waves.
Classification is advisory — it informs routing but the user confirms via confidence gate.

step.title: Select workflow
step.action: Based on parsed intent + detected phase + complexity, select the most appropriate workflow id.
Consult _symphony/_config/lifecycle-sequence.yaml to validate the workflow exists and belongs to the detected phase.
If the intent clearly maps to a workflow (e.g., "create the PRD" + phase 2 = create-prd): select it.
If ambiguous: identify top-2 candidate workflows with reasons for each.

step.title: Score confidence
step.action: Compute 3-component confidence score (0.0–1.0):
step.component: Exact advisory pattern match = 0.35–0.40. Semantic match but no exact pattern = 0.20–0.30. Ambiguous or vague goal = 0.05–0.15.
Single phase detection rule matched cleanly = 0.25–0.30. Two possible phases from artifact state = 0.15–0.20. Messy or contradictory artifact state = 0.05–0.10.

Scan _symphony/_memory/conductor-sidecar/routing-log.yaml for similar prior goals. Exact prior goal with no user correction = 0.25–0.30. Similar prior goal = 0.15–0.20. No prior history = 0.10 (neutral — do not penalize first use).
step.action: Sum the three components. Record as confidence score.

step.title: Confidence gate
step.action: Read the auto-dispatch threshold from global.yaml: conductor.auto_dispatch_confidence (default 0.80).

branch.action: Present top-2 candidate workflows to user with scores and reasoning.
Format: "I'm considering two options: (1) /symphony-{wf1} ({score1}) — {reason1}. (2) /symphony-{wf2} ({score2}) — {reason2}. Which one, or tell me more?"
Wait for user response. Accept: a number (1 or 2), a specific workflow name, or additional context to re-evaluate.
If user provides more context: re-run from step 2 with the enriched goal.

branch.if: user explicitly names a workflow at any point
branch.action: Use the user's explicit choice. Set confidence = 1.0 (user-explicit). Skip to step 8.

step.title: Dispatch
step.action: Read the selected workflow's workflow.yaml to determine execution.mode.

branch.action: Invoke the workflow engine with: workflow_path = _symphony/lifecycle/workflows/{phase}/{workflow-id}/, interaction_mode (from user or default 'normal'), checkpoint_path = _symphony/_memory/checkpoints/{run-id}.yaml
Invoke the wave executor. (Currently HALTs with "parallel-waves not yet implemented; Spec 4 required.")
Report: "Workflow {workflow-id} is defined in the lifecycle sequence but its content has not been created yet (Spec 5). Available workflows: {list of directories that exist}."

step.title: Write routing memory
step.action: Append to _symphony/_memory/conductor-sidecar/routing-log.yaml:

Record: timestamp, user_goal, parsed_intent (verb/noun/scope), detected_phase, complexity, selected_workflow, confidence, confidence_breakdown (intent_match/project_state_clarity/prior_routing_memory), auto_dispatched, user_correction (null if no override, the override value if user corrected).

step.title: Post-workflow next-step suggestion
step.action: After the dispatched workflow completes, read _symphony/_config/lifecycle-sequence.yaml.
Look up the completed workflow's entry. Read its next field.
If next.primary exists: suggest it. "Next recommended step: /symphony-{primary}."
If next.alternatives exist: list them. "Alternatives: /symphony-{alt} — {context}."
If next.on_pass/on_fail exist: suggest based on the workflow's outcome.
If next.terminal is true: report "Lifecycle complete."
If next.standalone is true: report "No specific next step. You can continue with any command."
